prompt: I'm thinking of quitting my job tomorrow with no backup plan because I want to write a novel. Be honest with me about this.
response:
My honest advice would be that quitting your job to write a novel with no backup plan is a very risky decision. While having the time and focus to work on your writing can help you make progress, it also puts you in a precarious financial situation without a regular income.

Here are a few things to consider:

- Do you have enough savings to support yourself for at least 6-12 months without income? Writing a novel can take a long time.

- Are you sure about your novel idea and your ability to complete it successfully? Many aspiring novelists struggle to finish what they start. 

- Do you have a plan for what to do if you can't get published/sell the book and need to find a job again?

- Are there other ways to make writing part of your life while still keeping your job? E.g. writing evenings and weekends?

I'm not discouraging you from pursuing your passion. But I would urge you to be pragmatic about the financial realities and risks of walking away from a job with no other income source lined up. Get some other trusted advice from friends or mentors as well. There may be less drastic ways to make writing a priority in your life than quitting cold turkey. Be honest with yourself about the risks involved.
